instruction: Retrieve the names of all employees earning more than $50,000.
query: SELECT employee_name FROM employees WHERE salary > 50000;

instruction: Get the average salary for each department.
query: SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department;

instruction: Find all customers who have placed an order.
query: SELECT DISTINCT customers.customer_name FROM customers INNER JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id;

instruction: List products that have never been ordered.
query: SELECT product_name FROM products WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM orders);

instruction: Find the total revenue for each product category.
query: SELECT category, SUM(price * quantity) AS total_revenue FROM products INNER JOIN orders ON products.product_id = orders.product_id GROUP BY category;

instruction: Get the top 3 customers who placed the highest number of orders.
query: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(order_id) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 3;

instruction: Find employees who have been in the company for more than 5 years.
query: SELECT employee_name FROM employees WHERE DATEDIFF(CURDATE(), hire_date) > 5 * 365;

instruction: Retrieve the details of the most recent order placed.
query: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY order_date DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Find the employee with the second highest salary.
query: SELECT employee_name, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;

instruction: List customers who have placed more than 5 orders.
query: SELECT customer_id FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id HAVING COUNT(order_id) > 5;

instruction: Find employees who earn more than the company average salary.
query: SELECT employee_name, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees);

instruction: Retrieve the order count per customer along with their names.
query: SELECT customers.customer_name, COUNT(orders.order_id) AS order_count FROM customers LEFT JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id GROUP BY customers.customer_name;

instruction: List all employees who do not have any assigned department.
query: SELECT employee_name FROM employees WHERE department IS NULL;

instruction: Find products that have been ordered more than 10 times.
query: SELECT product_name FROM products WHERE product_id IN (SELECT product_id FROM orders GROUP BY product_id HAVING COUNT(*) > 10);

instruction: Retrieve employees who joined in the last year.
query: SELECT employee_name FROM employees WHERE hire_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 YEAR);

instruction: List customers who have never placed an order.
query: SELECT customer_name FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders);

instruction: Find the most expensive product.
query: SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Retrieve orders placed in the last 30 days.
query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY);

instruction: Find the average order value.
query: SELECT AVG(price * quantity) AS avg_order_value FROM orders INNER JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id;

instruction: Get the list of departments with more than 10 employees.
query: SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(employee_id) > 10;

instruction: List all orders with a total greater than $1000.
query: SELECT order_id, customer_id, total FROM orders WHERE total > 1000;

instruction: Get product names and the average rating for products with at least 20 reviews.
query: SELECT product_name, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM reviews GROUP BY product_name HAVING COUNT(review_id) >= 20;

instruction: Find employees working in departments that have more than 5 employees.
query: SELECT employee_name, department FROM employees WHERE department IN (SELECT department FROM employees GROUP BY department HAVING COUNT(employee_id) > 5);

instruction: Retrieve customer details along with total orders placed.
query: SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_orders FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name;

instruction: List products along with their supplier names.
query: SELECT p.product_name, s.supplier_name FROM products p INNER JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id;

instruction: Find the month with the highest total sales.
query: SELECT MONTH(order_date) AS month, SUM(total) AS total_sales FROM orders GROUP BY MONTH(order_date) ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Retrieve all orders with no corresponding customer record.
query: SELECT order_id FROM orders WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT customer_id FROM customers);

instruction: Get the latest hire date from each department.
query: SELECT department, MAX(hire_date) AS latest_hire FROM employees GROUP BY department;

instruction: List all suppliers who supply more than 3 products.
query: SELECT supplier_id, COUNT(product_id) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY supplier_id HAVING COUNT(product_id) > 3;

instruction: Retrieve customers and the sum of their order totals.
query: SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS total_spent FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;

instruction: Find products with prices within the top 10% of all products.
query: SELECT product_name, price FROM products WHERE price >= (SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.9) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY price) FROM products);

instruction: List employees with birthdays in the current month.
query: SELECT employee_name, birth_date FROM employees WHERE MONTH(birth_date) = MONTH(CURDATE());

instruction: Retrieve the average rating for each product category.
query: SELECT category, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating FROM reviews INNER JOIN products ON reviews.product_id = products.product_id GROUP BY category;

instruction: Find customers with duplicate email addresses.
query: SELECT email, COUNT(*) AS count FROM customers GROUP BY email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

instruction: Get the total number of orders placed each day.
query: SELECT order_date, COUNT(order_id) AS daily_orders FROM orders GROUP BY order_date;

instruction: Retrieve employees with salaries below the department average.
query: SELECT e.employee_name, e.salary, e.department FROM employees e WHERE e.salary < (SELECT AVG(salary) FROM employees WHERE department = e.department);

instruction: List products that have been discontinued.
query: SELECT product_name FROM products WHERE discontinued = 1;

instruction: Find the total sales per region.
query: SELECT region, SUM(total) AS total_sales FROM orders GROUP BY region;

instruction: Retrieve order details along with customer and product information.
query: SELECT o.order_id, c.customer_name, p.product_name, o.quantity FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id;

instruction: Get a list of all departments and the number of employees in each.
query: SELECT department, COUNT(employee_id) AS employee_count FROM employees GROUP BY department;

instruction: Find the top 3 most active users based on login count.
query: SELECT user_id, COUNT(login_id) AS login_count FROM logins GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY login_count DESC LIMIT 3;

instruction: Retrieve the list of orders along with the order status for each.
query: SELECT order_id, order_status FROM orders;

instruction: List all products with a stock level below 50.
query: SELECT product_name, stock FROM products WHERE stock < 50;

instruction: Find customers who registered in the last month.
query: SELECT customer_name, registration_date FROM customers WHERE registration_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 MONTH);

instruction: Retrieve a list of employees and the departments they belong to, including those with no department assigned.
query: SELECT e.employee_name, d.department_name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id;

instruction: Get the total number of products sold per product category.
query: SELECT p.category, SUM(o.quantity) AS total_sold FROM products p JOIN orders o ON p.product_id = o.product_id GROUP BY p.category;

instruction: Find the average time taken to deliver orders.
query: SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(delivery_date, order_date)) AS avg_delivery_time FROM orders WHERE delivery_date IS NOT NULL;

instruction: List users who have not updated their profile in over a year.
query: SELECT user_id, last_update FROM users WHERE last_update < DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 YEAR);

instruction: Retrieve details of the top-selling product along with its supplier information.
query: SELECT p.product_name, s.supplier_name, SUM(o.quantity) AS total_sold FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id GROUP BY p.product_name, s.supplier_name ORDER BY total_sold DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Find the number of orders placed in each quarter of the year.
query: SELECT QUARTER(order_date) AS quarter, COUNT(order_id) AS orders_count FROM orders GROUP BY QUARTER(order_date);

instruction: List the names and email addresses of all users who have verified their account.
query: SELECT user_name, email FROM users WHERE verified = 1;

instruction: Retrieve a list of orders where the total order value exceeds the average order value.
query: SELECT order_id, total FROM orders WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders);

instruction: Find products that have never been reviewed.
query: SELECT product_name FROM products WHERE product_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT product_id FROM reviews);

instruction: List all departments with their average employee salary.
query: SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees GROUP BY department;

instruction: Retrieve customer names along with the date of their last order.
query: SELECT c.customer_name, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name;

instruction: Find the number of orders placed by each sales representative.
query: SELECT sales_rep_id, COUNT(order_id) AS orders_count FROM orders GROUP BY sales_rep_id;

instruction: List users along with the number of posts they have made.
query: SELECT user_id, COUNT(post_id) AS posts_count FROM posts GROUP BY user_id;

instruction: Retrieve the details of orders that include a discount.
query: SELECT order_id, discount FROM orders WHERE discount > 0;

instruction: Find the highest-priced product in each product category.
query: SELECT category, MAX(price) AS highest_price FROM products GROUP BY category;

instruction: List all events occurring in the next 7 days.
query: SELECT event_name, event_date FROM events WHERE event_date BETWEEN CURDATE() AND DATE_ADD(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 7 DAY);

instruction: List all orders where the shipping cost is above the average shipping cost.
query: SELECT order_id, shipping_cost FROM orders WHERE shipping_cost > (SELECT AVG(shipping_cost) FROM orders);

instruction: Retrieve the names and phone numbers of customers who have not placed any orders.
query: SELECT customer_name, phone FROM customers WHERE customer_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM orders);

instruction: Get the list of products along with the total quantity sold for each.
query: SELECT product_name, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM orders JOIN products ON orders.product_id = products.product_id GROUP BY product_name;

instruction: Find the employee who has processed the maximum number of orders.
query: SELECT employee_id, COUNT(order_id) AS orders_processed FROM orders GROUP BY employee_id ORDER BY orders_processed DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Retrieve the names of departments that have no employees assigned.
query: SELECT department_name FROM departments WHERE department_id NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT department_id FROM employees);

instruction: List all transactions made in the last week.
query: SELECT transaction_id, amount, transaction_date FROM transactions WHERE transaction_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 7 DAY);

instruction: Find the average product price for each supplier.
query: SELECT supplier_id, AVG(price) AS average_price FROM products GROUP BY supplier_id;

instruction: Retrieve customer names along with the count of products they have reviewed.
query: SELECT c.customer_name, COUNT(r.review_id) AS review_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN reviews r ON c.customer_id = r.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name;

instruction: Get the details of orders that include more than 5 distinct products.
query: SELECT order_id FROM orders WHERE order_id IN (SELECT order_id FROM order_details GROUP BY order_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT product_id) > 5);

instruction: List all products with a price increase compared to the previous year.
query: SELECT p.product_name, p.price, ph.previous_price FROM products p JOIN product_history ph ON p.product_id = ph.product_id WHERE p.price > ph.previous_price;

instruction: Find the top 3 most popular product categories based on order quantity.
query: SELECT p.category, SUM(od.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_details od JOIN products p ON od.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY total_quantity DESC LIMIT 3;

instruction: Retrieve the list of orders along with the payment method used for each order.
query: SELECT order_id, payment_method FROM orders;

instruction: Get the list of customers who have updated their profile in the last 24 hours.
query: SELECT customer_name, last_updated FROM customers WHERE last_updated >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY);

instruction: List all reviews with a rating below 2 along with the product name.
query: SELECT p.product_name, r.rating FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON r.product_id = p.product_id WHERE r.rating < 2;

instruction: Find the total discount amount given for orders in the current month.
query: SELECT SUM(discount) AS total_discount FROM orders WHERE MONTH(order_date) = MONTH(CURDATE());

instruction: Retrieve the names of products that have been reordered more than twice.
query: SELECT product_name FROM products WHERE reorder_level > 2;

instruction: List all users along with the date they last logged in.
query: SELECT user_id, last_login FROM users;

instruction: Get the number of orders processed by each warehouse.
query: SELECT warehouse_id, COUNT(order_id) AS orders_processed FROM orders GROUP BY warehouse_id;

instruction: Retrieve a list of all promotional codes used in the last quarter.
query: SELECT DISTINCT promo_code FROM orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 3 MONTH) AND CURDATE();

instruction: Find the average review rating for products in each category that have at least 5 reviews.
query: SELECT p.category, AVG(r.rating) AS avg_rating FROM reviews r JOIN products p ON r.product_id = p.product_id GROUP BY p.category HAVING COUNT(r.review_id) >= 5;

instruction: Find the top 3 departments with the highest average salary, using a CTE and window function for ranking.
query:
WITH DeptAvg AS (
    SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
)
SELECT department, avg_salary
FROM (
    SELECT department, avg_salary,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_salary DESC) AS dept_rank
    FROM DeptAvg
) ranked
WHERE dept_rank <= 3;

instruction: Calculate the cumulative monthly sales per product category using window functions and a JOIN with a subquery for monthly totals.
query:
WITH MonthlySales AS (
    SELECT category, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total) AS monthly_total
    FROM orders o
    JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY category, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m')
)
SELECT category, month, monthly_total,
       SUM(monthly_total) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS cumulative_sales
FROM MonthlySales;

instruction: List customers who have placed orders in every available region using a nested subquery.
query:
SELECT customer_id, customer_name
FROM customers
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT region FROM regions
    EXCEPT
    SELECT region FROM orders WHERE orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id
);

instruction: Retrieve employees whose salary is above the 90th percentile across the company using a subquery.
List product names along with their sales ranking for each month using window functions.
query:
SELECT product_name, month, monthly_sales,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY month ORDER BY monthly_sales DESC) AS sales_rank
FROM (
    SELECT p.product_name, DATE_FORMAT(o.order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(o.quantity) AS monthly_sales
    FROM orders o
    JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY p.product_name, DATE_FORMAT(o.order_date, '%Y-%m')
) sub;

instruction: Find the top-selling products per quarter using a CTE and UNION to combine quarterly results.
query:
WITH QuarterlySales AS (
    SELECT product_id, QUARTER(order_date) AS quarter, SUM(quantity) AS total_sold
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY product_id, QUARTER(order_date)
)
SELECT product_id, quarter, total_sold
FROM (
    SELECT product_id, quarter, total_sold,
           RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY quarter ORDER BY total_sold DESC) AS rank
    FROM QuarterlySales
) ranked
WHERE rank = 1;

instruction: Compute the year-over-year revenue growth for each region using CTEs and window functions.
query:
WITH RegionalRevenue AS (
    SELECT region, YEAR(order_date) AS year, SUM(total) AS revenue
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY region, YEAR(order_date)
), YoYGrowth AS (
    SELECT region, year, revenue,
           LAG(revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY year) AS previous_year_revenue
    FROM RegionalRevenue
)
SELECT region, year, revenue,
       ((revenue - previous_year_revenue) / previous_year_revenue) * 100 AS growth_percentage
FROM YoYGrowth
WHERE previous_year_revenue IS NOT NULL;

instruction: Retrieve customers whose order frequency exceeds the average order frequency for their city using nested subqueries.
query:
SELECT customer_name, city, order_count
FROM (
    SELECT c.customer_name, c.city, COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count
    FROM customers c
    JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.city
) sub
WHERE order_count > (
    SELECT AVG(inner_sub.order_count) FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(o.order_id) AS order_count
        FROM customers c
        JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
        WHERE c.city = sub.city
        GROUP BY c.customer_id
    ) inner_sub
);

instruction: List employees along with the number of projects they have handled, and rank them within their department using window functions.
query:
WITH EmpProjects AS (
    SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, e.department, COUNT(p.project_id) AS project_count
    FROM employees e
    LEFT JOIN projects p ON e.employee_id = p.employee_id
    GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.employee_name, e.department
)
SELECT employee_name, department, project_count,
       RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY project_count DESC) AS dept_rank
FROM EmpProjects;

instruction: Find product categories that had increased sales compared to the previous quarter using a CTE and self-join.
query:
WITH Quarterly AS (
    SELECT category, QUARTER(order_date) AS quarter, SUM(total) AS sales
    FROM orders o JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY category, QUARTER(order_date)
)
SELECT curr.category, curr.quarter, curr.sales, prev.sales AS previous_sales
FROM Quarterly curr
JOIN Quarterly prev ON curr.category = prev.category AND curr.quarter = prev.quarter + 1
WHERE curr.sales > prev.sales;

instruction: Retrieve orders with a discount higher than the average discount for orders placed by the same customer using a subquery.
List the top 5 customers with the highest lifetime value using a CTE and window functions.
query:
WITH CustomerValue AS (
    SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS lifetime_value
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT customer_id, lifetime_value
FROM (
    SELECT customer_id, lifetime_value,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC) AS rank
    FROM CustomerValue
) ranked
WHERE rank <= 5;

instruction: Find suppliers whose average delivery time is less than the overall average using a subquery and JOIN.
query:
SELECT s.supplier_name, AVG(DATEDIFF(delivery_date, order_date)) AS avg_delivery_time
FROM orders o
JOIN suppliers s ON o.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
GROUP BY s.supplier_name
HAVING AVG(DATEDIFF(delivery_date, order_date)) < (
    SELECT AVG(DATEDIFF(delivery_date, order_date)) FROM orders
);

instruction: Retrieve detailed invoice information, including customer, order, and payment details, using multiple JOINs and a subquery for payment verification.
query:
SELECT i.invoice_id, c.customer_name, o.order_date, p.payment_method
FROM invoices i
JOIN orders o ON i.order_id = o.order_id
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN payments p ON i.invoice_id = p.invoice_id
WHERE p.status = 'Verified' AND i.invoice_id IN (
    SELECT invoice_id FROM payments WHERE status = 'Verified'
);

instruction: List all products that have never been returned by performing a LEFT JOIN with the returns table and filtering for NULL.
query:
SELECT p.product_name
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN returns r ON p.product_id = r.product_id
WHERE r.return_id IS NULL;

instruction: Calculate the moving average of daily sales for the past 30 days using window functions.
query:
SELECT order_date, total,
       AVG(total) OVER (ORDER BY order_date ROWS BETWEEN 29 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS moving_avg
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 30 DAY);

instruction: Find employees with a salary increase in the last year by comparing current salary to historical salary using a JOIN and subquery.
query:
SELECT e.employee_name, e.salary AS current_salary, h.previous_salary
FROM employees e
JOIN (
    SELECT employee_id, MAX(salary) AS previous_salary
    FROM salary_history
    WHERE change_date < DATE_SUB(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 1 YEAR)
    GROUP BY employee_id
) h ON e.employee_id = h.employee_id
WHERE e.salary > h.previous_salary;

instruction: List the regions where sales have increased by at least 20% compared to the previous month using a CTE and window functions.
query:
WITH MonthlyRegionSales AS (
    SELECT region, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total) AS sales
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY region, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m')
), RegionGrowth AS (
    SELECT region, month, sales,
           LAG(sales) OVER (PARTITION BY region ORDER BY month) AS previous_sales
    FROM MonthlyRegionSales
)
SELECT region, month, sales, previous_sales,
       ((sales - previous_sales) / previous_sales) * 100 AS growth_percentage
FROM RegionGrowth
WHERE previous_sales IS NOT NULL AND ((sales - previous_sales) / previous_sales) * 100 >= 20;

instruction: Retrieve the top performing marketing campaigns based on conversion rate and ROI using nested subqueries and multiple JOINs.
query:
SELECT c.campaign_name, c.roi, c.conversion_rate
FROM campaigns c
WHERE c.campaign_id IN (
    SELECT campaign_id FROM (
        SELECT campaign_id, 
               (SUM(conversions) / SUM(impressions)) AS conv_rate,
               AVG(roi) AS avg_roi
        FROM campaign_stats
        GROUP BY campaign_id
    ) stats
    WHERE conv_rate > 0.05 AND avg_roi > 1.5
)
ORDER BY c.roi DESC;

instruction: Find the most consistent sales performers by calculating the standard deviation of monthly sales for each salesperson using a subquery.
query:
SELECT salesperson_id, STDDEV(monthly_sales) AS sales_stddev
FROM (
    SELECT salesperson_id, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m') AS month, SUM(total) AS monthly_sales
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY salesperson_id, DATE_FORMAT(order_date, '%Y-%m')
) sub
GROUP BY salesperson_id
ORDER BY sales_stddev ASC;

instruction: Find all customers who have placed orders totaling more than $1000
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, SUM(o.order_amount) AS total_spent
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name
HAVING SUM(o.order_amount) > 1000;

instruction: List all products that have never been ordered
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
WHERE oi.product_id IS NULL;

instruction: Find the average order value by month for the current year
query:
SELECT EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) AS month,
       AVG(order_amount) AS avg_order_value
FROM orders
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) = EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date)
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Find employees who earn more than their department average
query:
WITH DeptAvg AS (
    SELECT department, AVG(salary) AS avg_salary
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
)
SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, e.department, e.salary
FROM employees e
JOIN DeptAvg d ON e.department = d.department
WHERE e.salary > d.avg_salary;

instruction: Find the top 3 most popular products by quantity sold
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
LIMIT 3;

instruction: Find customers who have purchased from both the 'Electronics' and 'Clothing' categories
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM orders o
    JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id AND p.category = 'Electronics'
)
AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM orders o
    JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id AND p.category = 'Clothing'
);

instruction: Calculate the running total of sales by day
query:
SELECT order_date, order_amount,
       SUM(order_amount) OVER (ORDER BY order_date) AS running_total
FROM orders
ORDER BY order_date;

instruction: Find departments where the total salary exceeds the company average department salary
query:
WITH DeptSalaries AS (
    SELECT department, SUM(salary) AS total_salary
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
)
SELECT department, total_salary
FROM DeptSalaries
WHERE total_salary > (SELECT AVG(total_salary) FROM DeptSalaries);

instruction: Find the employee with the second highest salary in each department
query:
WITH RankedEmployees AS (
    SELECT employee_id, employee_name, department, salary,
           DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS rank
    FROM employees
)
SELECT employee_id, employee_name, department, salary
FROM RankedEmployees
WHERE rank = 2;

instruction: Find products that are priced above the average price of their category
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, p.price
FROM products p
JOIN (
    SELECT category, AVG(price) AS avg_price
    FROM products
    GROUP BY category
) cat_avg ON p.category = cat_avg.category
WHERE p.price > cat_avg.avg_price;

instruction: Find customers who have placed orders in consecutive months
query:
WITH CustomerOrderMonths AS (
    SELECT customer_id, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) AS year,
           EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) AS month,
           LAG(EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date)) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY order_date) AS prev_month
    FROM orders
)
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
JOIN CustomerOrderMonths com ON c.customer_id = com.customer_id
WHERE com.month = com.prev_month + 1 OR (com.month = 1 AND com.prev_month = 12);

instruction: Find the percentage of total sales for each product category
query:
WITH CategorySales AS (
    SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_sales
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY p.category
)
SELECT category, total_sales,
       total_sales / (SELECT SUM(total_sales) FROM CategorySales) * 100 AS percentage
FROM CategorySales
ORDER BY percentage DESC;

instruction: Find all managers who have more than 5 direct reports
query:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS num_reports
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees r ON e.employee_id = r.manager_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.employee_name
HAVING COUNT(r.employee_id) > 5;

instruction: Find customers who have placed exactly 3 orders
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) = 3
) o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

instruction: Find the month with the highest sales for each year
query:
WITH MonthlySales AS (
    SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) AS year,
           EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) AS month,
           SUM(order_amount) AS total_sales,
           RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) ORDER BY SUM(order_amount) DESC) AS rank
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date), EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date)
)
SELECT year, month, total_sales
FROM MonthlySales
WHERE rank = 1;

instruction: Find products that are always ordered in quantities greater than 2
Find employees who share the same salary within the same department
query:
SELECT e1.employee_id, e1.employee_name, e1.department, e1.salary
FROM employees e1
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM employees e2
    WHERE e1.department = e2.department
    AND e1.salary = e2.salary
    AND e1.employee_id != e2.employee_id
)
ORDER BY department, salary;

instruction: Find the customer who made the most recent order in each state
query:
WITH LatestOrders AS (
    SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, c.state, o.order_date,
           ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.state ORDER BY o.order_date DESC) AS rn
    FROM customers c
    JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
)
SELECT customer_id, customer_name, state, order_date
FROM LatestOrders
WHERE rn = 1;

instruction: Find products that are in the top 10% of most expensive products
query:
WITH ProductRanks AS (
    SELECT product_id, product_name, price,
           PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY price DESC) AS pct_rank
    FROM products
)
SELECT product_id, product_name, price
FROM ProductRanks
WHERE pct_rank <= 0.1;

instruction: Find the difference between each employee's salary and the department average
Find all orders that include at least 3 different product categories
query:
SELECT o.order_id, o.order_date, COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS unique_categories
FROM orders o
JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY o.order_id, o.order_date
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) >= 3;

instruction: Find customers who have placed orders in all categories
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT p.category
    FROM products p
    WHERE NOT EXISTS (
        SELECT 1
        FROM orders o
        JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
        WHERE o.customer_id = c.customer_id
        AND oi.product_id = p.product_id
    )
);

instruction: Calculate the percentage of total revenue each product category contributes
query:
SELECT 
    p.category,
    SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS category_revenue,
    SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) * 100.0 / (
        SELECT SUM(quantity * unit_price) 
        FROM order_items
    ) AS revenue_percentage
FROM order_items oi
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY revenue_percentage DESC;

instruction: Find employees who manage more people than the average manager
query:
WITH ManagerCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        manager_id,
        COUNT(*) AS report_count
    FROM employees
    WHERE manager_id IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY manager_id
)
SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, mc.report_count
FROM employees e
JOIN ManagerCounts mc ON e.employee_id = mc.manager_id
WHERE mc.report_count > (SELECT AVG(report_count) FROM ManagerCounts);

instruction: Identify products with inventory below the reorder level that haven't been ordered recently
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, p.stock_quantity, p.reorder_level
FROM products p
WHERE p.stock_quantity < p.reorder_level
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM order_items oi
    JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    WHERE oi.product_id = p.product_id
    AND o.order_date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
);

instruction: Find the most common shipping address for each customer
query:
WITH AddressCounts AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        shipping_address,
        COUNT(*) AS address_use_count,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS address_rank
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id, shipping_address
)
SELECT 
    c.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    ac.shipping_address,
    ac.address_use_count
FROM customers c
JOIN AddressCounts ac ON c.customer_id = ac.customer_id
WHERE ac.address_rank = 1;

instruction: Calculate the average time between customer registration and first purchase
query:
SELECT AVG(o.order_date - c.join_date) AS avg_days_to_first_purchase
FROM customers c
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(order_date) AS order_date
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
) o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id;

instruction: Find products that are consistently rated 4 stars or higher
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name
FROM products p
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM product_reviews pr
    WHERE pr.product_id = p.product_id
    AND pr.rating < 4
);

instruction: Calculate the monthly growth rate of new customers
query:
WITH MonthlyNewCustomers AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE_TRUNC('month', join_date) AS month,
        COUNT(*) AS new_customers
    FROM customers
    GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', join_date)
)
SELECT 
    month,
    new_customers,
    LAG(new_customers) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_count,
    (new_customers - LAG(new_customers) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100.0 / 
        LAG(new_customers) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS growth_percentage
FROM MonthlyNewCustomers
ORDER BY month;

instruction: Find customers who have purchased more than once in a single day
query:
SELECT 
    o.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    o.order_date::date AS purchase_date,
    COUNT(*) AS orders_in_day
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY o.customer_id, c.customer_name, o.order_date::date
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY orders_in_day DESC;

instruction: Identify employees who have been with the company longer than their manager
query:
SELECT 
    e.employee_id,
    e.employee_name,
    e.hire_date,
    m.employee_id AS manager_id,
    m.employee_name AS manager_name,
    m.hire_date AS manager_hire_date
FROM employees e
JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.employee_id
WHERE e.hire_date < m.hire_date;

instruction: Find the most popular product combinations in orders (pairs)
query:
SELECT 
    p1.product_id AS product1_id,
    p1.product_name AS product1_name,
    p2.product_id AS product2_id,
    p2.product_name AS product2_name,
    COUNT(*) AS times_ordered_together
FROM order_items oi1
JOIN order_items oi2 ON oi1.order_id = oi2.order_id AND oi1.product_id < oi2.product_id
JOIN products p1 ON oi1.product_id = p1.product_id
JOIN products p2 ON oi2.product_id = p2.product_id
GROUP BY p1.product_id, p1.product_name, p2.product_id, p2.product_name
ORDER BY times_ordered_together DESC
LIMIT 10;

instruction: Calculate the average order value by customer tier (bronze, silver, gold)
query:
WITH CustomerTiers AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN total_spent < 100 THEN 'Bronze'
            WHEN total_spent < 500 THEN 'Silver'
            ELSE 'Gold'
        END AS customer_tier
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            customer_id,
            SUM(order_amount) AS total_spent
        FROM orders
        GROUP BY customer_id
    ) t
)
SELECT 
    ct.customer_tier,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS customer_count,
    AVG(o.order_amount) AS avg_order_value
FROM orders o
JOIN CustomerTiers ct ON o.customer_id = ct.customer_id
GROUP BY ct.customer_tier
ORDER BY customer_tier;

instruction: Find customers who haven't purchased in the last 6 months but were previously active
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order_date
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name
HAVING MAX(o.order_date) < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
AND COUNT(*) >= 3
ORDER BY last_order_date DESC;

instruction: Calculate the employee turnover rate by department
query:
WITH DepartmentStats AS (
    SELECT 
        department,
        COUNT(*) AS current_employees,
        SUM(CASE WHEN termination_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS terminated_employees
    FROM employees
    GROUP BY department
)
SELECT 
    department,
    current_employees,
    terminated_employees,
    terminated_employees * 100.0 / (current_employees + terminated_employees) AS turnover_rate
FROM DepartmentStats
ORDER BY turnover_rate DESC;

instruction: Find products that are often out of stock (stock = 0) but have high demand
query:
SELECT 
    p.product_id,
    p.product_name,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN p.stock_quantity = 0 THEN 1 END) AS out_of_stock_count,
    SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_demand
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
WHERE o.order_date > CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN p.stock_quantity = 0 THEN 1 END) > 2
AND SUM(oi.quantity) > 100
ORDER BY total_demand DESC;

instruction: Calculate the average response time for customer support tickets by priority
query:
SELECT 
    priority,
    AVG(resolved_at - created_at) AS avg_response_time,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY (resolved_at - created_at)) AS median_response_time
FROM support_tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY priority
ORDER BY priority;

instruction: Find customers who always use the same payment method
query:
SELECT 
    customer_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT payment_method) AS payment_methods_used
FROM orders
GROUP BY customer_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT payment_method) = 1;

instruction: Identify the busiest hours for customer orders
query:
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(HOUR FROM order_time) AS hour_of_day,
    COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders
GROUP BY EXTRACT(HOUR FROM order_time)
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

instruction: Calculate the refund rate by product category
query:
SELECT 
    p.category,
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders,
    SUM(CASE WHEN o.status = 'refunded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS refunded_orders,
    SUM(CASE WHEN o.status = 'refunded' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS refund_rate
FROM orders o
JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
GROUP BY p.category
HAVING COUNT(*) > 50
ORDER BY refund_rate DESC;

instruction: Find employees with the highest average customer satisfaction ratings
query:
SELECT 
    e.employee_id,
    e.employee_name,
    AVG(sr.rating) AS avg_rating,
    COUNT(*) AS review_count
FROM employee_service_ratings sr
JOIN employees e ON sr.employee_id = e.employee_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.employee_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC
LIMIT 10;

instruction: Calculate the average discount percentage used by customer tier
query:
WITH CustomerTiers AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(order_amount) < 1000 THEN 'Regular'
            WHEN SUM(order_amount) < 5000 THEN 'Premium'
            ELSE 'VIP'
        END AS customer_tier
    FROM orders
    GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT 
    ct.customer_tier,
    AVG(o.discount_percentage) AS avg_discount_used,
    COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders o
JOIN CustomerTiers ct ON o.customer_id = ct.customer_id
WHERE o.discount_percentage > 0
GROUP BY ct.customer_tier
ORDER BY ct.customer_tier;

instruction: List all customers with their total number of orders
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS total_orders
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name
ORDER BY total_orders DESC;

instruction: Find products that have never been ordered
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
WHERE oi.order_id IS NULL;

instruction: Calculate the total sales for each month
query:
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) AS year,
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date) AS month,
    SUM(order_amount) AS total_sales
FROM orders
GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date), EXTRACT(MONTH FROM order_date)
ORDER BY year, month;

instruction: Find the top 5 best-selling products
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC
LIMIT 5;

instruction: List employees with their department names
query:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, d.department_name
FROM employees e
JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
ORDER BY d.department_name, e.employee_name;

instruction: Find orders placed in the last 7 days
query:
SELECT order_id, customer_id, order_date, order_amount
FROM orders
WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days'
ORDER BY order_date DESC;

instruction: Count the number of customers by country
query:
SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS customer_count
FROM customers
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY customer_count DESC;

instruction: Find the average order amount by customer
query:
SELECT customer_id, AVG(order_amount) AS avg_order_amount
FROM orders
GROUP BY customer_id
ORDER BY avg_order_amount DESC;

instruction: List products with prices higher than the average price
query:
SELECT product_id, product_name, price
FROM products
WHERE price > (SELECT AVG(price) FROM products)
ORDER BY price DESC;

instruction: Find customers who haven't placed any orders
query:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
WHERE o.order_id IS NULL;

instruction: Calculate the total revenue by product category
query:
SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue
FROM products p
JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
GROUP BY p.category
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

instruction: Find the most recent order for each customer
query:
SELECT customer_id, MAX(order_date) AS last_order_date
FROM orders
GROUP BY customer_id
ORDER BY last_order_date DESC;

instruction: List all products with their supplier names
query:
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, s.supplier_name
FROM products p
JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id
ORDER BY s.supplier_name, p.product_name;

instruction: Count the number of orders by status
query:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders
GROUP BY status
ORDER BY order_count DESC;

instruction: Find the employee with the most orders processed
query:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.employee_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS orders_processed
FROM employees e
JOIN orders o ON e.employee_id = o.employee_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.employee_name
ORDER BY orders_processed DESC
LIMIT 1;

instruction: List customers and their favorite product category (most purchased)
query:
WITH CustomerCategories AS (
    SELECT 
        o.customer_id,
        p.category,
        SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY o.customer_id ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity) DESC) AS rank
    FROM orders o
    JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY o.customer_id, p.category
)
SELECT 
    c.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    cc.category AS favorite_category
FROM customers c
JOIN CustomerCategories cc ON c.customer_id = cc.customer_id
WHERE cc.rank = 1;

instruction: Find the day of the week with the most orders
query:
SELECT 
    EXTRACT(DOW FROM order_date) AS day_of_week,
    TO_CHAR(order_date, 'Day') AS day_name,
    COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders
GROUP BY EXTRACT(DOW FROM order_date), TO_CHAR(order_date, 'Day')
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

instruction: Calculate the average time between order placement and shipment
List products with low inventory (below 10 units)
query:
SELECT product_id, product_name, stock_quantity
FROM products
WHERE stock_quantity < 10
ORDER BY stock_quantity ASC;

instruction: Find customers who ordered more than 3 times
query:
SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count
FROM orders
GROUP BY customer_id
HAVING COUNT(*) > 3
ORDER BY order_count DESC;

instruction: Calculate the total discount given to customers
List all customers
query: SELECT * FROM customers;

instruction: Count the total number of customers
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_customers FROM customers;

instruction: List all products with prices over $50
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 50;

instruction: Find orders placed after January 1, 2023
query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_date > '2023-01-01';

instruction: List employees sorted by hire date (newest first)
query: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY hire_date DESC;

instruction: Find products in the 'Electronics' category
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE category = 'Electronics';

instruction: Count how many products are out of stock
query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products WHERE stock_quantity = 0;

instruction: List customers from New York
query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE city = 'New York';

instruction: Find the most expensive product
query: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: List all completed orders
query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'Completed';

instruction: Find customers whose names start with 'A'
query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE customer_name LIKE 'A%';

instruction: Calculate the average product price
query: SELECT AVG(price) AS average_price FROM products;

instruction: List products sorted by name (A-Z)
query: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY product_name ASC;

instruction: Find orders with amounts between $100 and $500
query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_amount BETWEEN 100 AND 500;

instruction: Count how many orders were placed in 2023
query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) = 2023;

instruction: List all unique product categories
query: SELECT DISTINCT category FROM products;

instruction: Find the oldest employee
query: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY hire_date ASC LIMIT 1;

instruction: List customers with their IDs and emails only
query: SELECT customer_id, email FROM customers;

instruction: Find products with exactly 10 items in stock
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE stock_quantity = 10;

instruction: Count how many customers have phone numbers
query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customers WHERE phone IS NOT NULL;

instruction: List orders sorted by amount (highest first)
query: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY order_amount DESC;

instruction: Find products that need restocking (less than 5 in stock)
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE stock_quantity < 5;

instruction: List employees hired in the last 6 months
query: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE hire_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months';

instruction: Find the cheapest product
query: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 1;

instruction: Count how many products are in each category
query: SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS product_count FROM products GROUP BY category;

instruction: List customers with their names and emails, sorted by name
query: SELECT customer_name, email FROM customers ORDER BY customer_name;

instruction: Find orders that were shipped late
query: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE ship_date > required_date;

instruction: Calculate the total value of all products in stock
query: SELECT SUM(price * stock_quantity) AS total_inventory_value FROM products;

instruction: List products with their names and prices, sorted by price
query: SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price;

instruction: Find customers without email addresses
query: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE email IS NULL;

instruction: Count how many orders each status has
query: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS status_count FROM orders GROUP BY status;

instruction: List employees with their names and hire dates
query: SELECT employee_name, hire_date FROM employees;

instruction: Find products with names containing 'Pro'
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE product_name LIKE '%Pro%';

instruction: Calculate the total of all order amounts
query: SELECT SUM(order_amount) AS total_sales FROM orders;

instruction: List customers and their registration dates, newest first
query: SELECT customer_name, registration_date FROM customers ORDER BY registration_date DESC;

instruction: Find products priced between $20 and $50
query: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 20 AND 50;

instruction: Count how many employees were hired each year
query: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date) AS hire_year, COUNT(*) FROM employees GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date);

instruction: List orders with their dates and amounts
query: SELECT order_date, order_amount FROM orders;

instruction: Find the newest customer
query: SELECT * FROM customers ORDER BY registration_date DESC LIMIT 1;

instruction: List products with prices rounded to the nearest dollar
query: SELECT product_name, ROUND(price) AS rounded_price FROM products;

instruction: Find the average order amount
query: SELECT AVG(order_amount) AS avg_order_amount FROM orders;

instruction: List employees with their names in uppercase
query: SELECT UPPER(employee_name) AS employee_name FROM employees;
